title: Programme Graduate Futures Insight and Action Packs
summary: Creates programme-level graduate futures insight packs that connect employability data, comparator intelligence and programme strategy, then turn that insight into action, profile and story products.
detailSummary:
This deliverable gives King's a stronger institutional and programme-level handle on graduate futures. It brings together employability outcomes, graduate destinations, benchmarking, student aspiration and readiness data, segmentation and programme strategy evidence into a reusable insight model. The purpose is not to rank programmes against each other, but to help departments and programme teams understand their students' graduate futures in context, compare meaningfully with relevant subject or sector peers, and examine how those patterns interact with the programme's own employability strategy.

The work will produce a confident data and insight pack prototype by September 2026, with data in place and presentation co-constructed with users. It will then support a faculty-led train-the-trainer model so programme or department teams can use the data as a provocation for structured sense-making. That process should result in programme Graduate Premium Profiles, Graduate Futures Action Plans and Story Packs that help programmes strengthen, evidence and celebrate their contribution to students' graduate futures.
problemSolved: King's does not yet have a consistent way for course teams to bring together graduate futures evidence, interpret it responsibly and use it to explain course value or decide where student support could be strengthened.
whatChanges: Programmes, departments and faculties gain reusable insight packs and structured sense-making tools that help them understand graduate futures data, reflect on programme strategy, identify actions for improvement and celebrate their graduate futures offer with evidence-informed confidence.

caseForChange.problem: King's holds a range of employability, outcomes, benchmarking and student insight data, but programmes, departments and faculties often find it difficult to access, interpret and use this data in actionable ways. Broad institutional averages and headline highly skilled employment figures do not give programme teams enough nuance about destinations, student aspirations, comparator context, marginal outcomes, segmentation or the relationship between outcomes and programme strategy.
caseForChange.opportunity: By creating reusable graduate futures insight packs and a structured faculty-led sense-making model, King's can help programmes understand their graduate futures position, identify where current strategy is working, surface hidden strengths, target support more intelligently and create credible material for programme enhancement, student articulation, open days and graduate premium evidence work.
caseForChange.whyNow: Graduate outcomes, highly skilled employment and evidence of student value are increasingly central to institutional accountability, student choice, TEF narratives, programme review, recruitment and the wider graduate premium agenda. King's needs a stronger and more usable institutional model before programme teams can consistently act on the data or communicate their graduate futures offer with confidence.
caseForChange.intendedChange: King's moves from fragmented or underused employability reporting to a coherent graduate futures intelligence and action model. Programme teams are able to interpret data in context, understand the interaction between outcomes and employability strategy, agree targeted actions and articulate their contribution to students' graduate futures in a way that is evidence-informed, developmental and celebratory.

ownership.benefitOwner: Heads of Department, with Mark Peace as institutional lead for graduate futures
contributors: Planning Analytics or relevant institutional data colleagues
Student recruitment and open day colleagues where Story Packs are activated

components.title: Data source inventory and governance
components.summary: Confirm the datasets, owners, caveats, suppression rules, availability and responsible-use principles needed for programme-level graduate futures insight.

components.title: Destination taxonomy and comparator framework
components.summary: Define how graduate, non-graduate, marginal, transitional and pathway destinations should be interpreted, and agree meaningful subject, sector and Russell Group comparator sets.

components.title: Power BI prototype and insight pack design
components.summary: Build a confident September 2026 prototype with data in place, Power BI presentation developing, and insight pack design co-constructed with programme and faculty users.

components.title: Programme strategy interaction layer
components.summary: Connect outcomes and destinations data to programme-level employability strategies, including curriculum, assessment, skills, careers, alumni, experiential learning and opportunity evidence.

components.title: Faculty train-the-trainer model
components.summary: Equip Vice Dean delegates, ADEs, faculty education leads and Careers faculty partners to facilitate programme sense-making locally without relying on central delivery for every programme.

components.title: Programme sense-making and outputs
components.summary: Use the insight packs to support programme or department conversations that produce Graduate Premium Profiles, Graduate Futures Action Plans and Graduate Futures Story Packs.

components.title: Institutional synthesis and feed-through
components.summary: Build a typology of graduate futures patterns, programme strategy types, strengths and support needs, then feed findings into evidence packs, market activation, curriculum enhancement and skills work.

benefits.title: Stronger institutional grip on graduate futures data
benefits.statement: King's can see patterns, nuances and segmentations in employability outcomes, destinations, aspirations and readiness more clearly, rather than relying on broad averages or isolated headline indicators.
benefits.beneficiary: King's Edge, Careers and Employability Service, faculties, departments and senior education leaders

benefits.title: Programme teams can understand and act on their graduate futures position
benefits.statement: Programme and department teams can interpret outcomes, destinations, aspirations and comparator intelligence in ways that support local enhancement and more intentional employability strategy.

benefits.title: Employability strategy becomes more explicit at programme level
benefits.statement: Programmes can identify how curriculum, assessment, skills, experiential learning, careers engagement, alumni connection and student support shape graduate futures, including where strong practice is currently hidden or implicit.

benefits.title: Improvement activity is better targeted
benefits.statement: Departments, faculties, Careers and King's Edge can focus support where the evidence suggests it is most needed or most likely to unlock value.
benefits.beneficiary: Heads of Department, faculty education leads, Careers and Employability Service and students

benefits.title: Programmes can celebrate their graduate futures contribution more confidently
benefits.statement: Programmes have credible, evidence-informed language and proof points for open days, recruitment, induction, advising and student-facing communications.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, applicants, students, recruitment teams and faculties

benefits.title: Graduate premium evidence work is better grounded
benefits.statement: The insight packs, profiles, action plans, story packs and institutional synthesis provide a stronger evidence base for graduate premium evidence packs, market activation, curriculum enhancement and skills-related work.
benefits.beneficiary: King's Edge, senior leaders, faculties, students and external audiences

outputs.title: Data source inventory
outputs.description: A controlled inventory of graduate futures data sources, including Graduate Outcomes, LEO, TEF and benchmarking data, internal enrolment supplement data, destinations data, student segmentation data, careers engagement or opportunity participation data where available, labour market and skills demand intelligence, and optional curriculum or VLE employability marker analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Data sources, owners, availability and update cycles are documented.
Data caveats, suppression issues and responsible-use constraints are identified.
Phase one data sources for the September 2026 prototype are confirmed.

outputs.title: Data caveat and interpretation guide
outputs.description: A guide for responsible interpretation of small numbers, suppression, volatility, benchmarking limits, highly skilled employment coding, marginal destinations, further study pathways and segmentation.
acceptanceCriteria: The guide explains where data can and cannot support interpretation.
The guide includes specific handling guidance for marginal and near-graduate destinations.
The guide is usable by faculty facilitators and programme teams.

outputs.title: Destination taxonomy and comparator framework
outputs.description: A framework for classifying destinations as graduate, non-graduate, marginal, transitional, pathway, further study or unclear, and for selecting meaningful comparator sets by subject, sector, Russell Group or other relevant peer group.
acceptanceCriteria: Destination categories are defined and tested against real data examples.
Marginal destinations, such as teaching assistant roles that may be pathway-adjacent, are handled explicitly.
Comparator principles support like-for-like external sense-making rather than crude internal ranking.

outputs.title: Power BI prototype and insight pack design
outputs.description: A confident September 2026 prototype that presents programme, department and faculty-level graduate futures data through Power BI or an equivalent insight pack model, with the data in place and the presentation model still being co-constructed with users.
acceptanceCriteria: The prototype is ready to use with real programme or department examples by September 2026.
Core data is available and presented through a usable prototype.
The prototype supports headline outcomes, destinations, comparator context, aspiration and readiness data, segmentation where viable and caveat guidance.

outputs.title: Programme Graduate Futures Insight Pack template
outputs.description: The reusable pack that programme teams use to understand outcomes, destinations, aspirations, readiness, comparator position, segmentation and programme strategy prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: The template separates data, interpretation prompts and caveats clearly.
The template can be used at programme, department or faculty level where data allows.
The template supports the later Graduate Premium Profile, Action Plan and Story Pack products.

outputs.title: Train-the-trainer facilitation toolkit
outputs.description: A facilitation model for Vice Dean delegates, ADEs, faculty education leads and Careers faculty partners to run programme sense-making locally.
acceptanceCriteria: The toolkit includes facilitator guidance, training materials, workshop prompts, caveat guidance and structured return templates.
Faculty facilitators understand how to handle data interpretation, marginal destinations and developmental framing.
The model does not depend on central facilitation for every programme.

outputs.title: Programme Graduate Premium Profile template
outputs.description: A template that captures the programme's evidence-informed account of how it supports students' graduate futures, including distinctive pathways, skills, curriculum features, experiential learning, support practices and disciplinary identity.
acceptanceCriteria: The template invites programmes to describe their graduate futures orientation in a celebratory but evidence-informed way.
The profile distinguishes evidence, interpretation and programme narrative.
Programme confirmation is sufficient for local use.

outputs.title: Programme Graduate Futures Action Plan template
outputs.description: A template that turns insight and reflection into agreed actions, owners, support needs, timescales and measures for programme-level enhancement.
acceptanceCriteria: Actions are linked to evidence or workshop findings.
Each action has an owner, support need, timeframe and monitoring point.
Follow-up remains internal to departments and faculties through existing planning routes.

outputs.title: Programme Graduate Futures Story Pack template
outputs.description: A template that helps programmes create evidence-informed open day, recruitment, induction, advising and student-facing language about their graduate futures offer.
acceptanceCriteria: The Story Pack includes proof points, destination highlights, pathway examples, skills messages and open day talking points.
The language is celebratory but remains grounded in evidence.
Programme confirmation is sufficient before use.

outputs.title: Institutional graduate futures typology and synthesis
outputs.description: A synthesis of recurring destination patterns, employability strategy types, hidden strengths, support needs, skills demand themes and areas requiring institutional infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Faculty returns are synthesised into institutional patterns and support needs.
Findings feed related King's Edge deliverables and curriculum enhancement activity.
The synthesis distinguishes evidence, interpretation and recommended institutional action.

measures.title: Data coverage and readiness
measures.questionAnswered: Have the key data sources needed for programme-level graduate futures insight been identified, assessed and made usable for the prototype?
measures.measure: Completion of data source inventory, caveat register and phase one data specification.
measures.baseline: Data exists across sources but is not yet presented through a coherent programme-level insight model.
measures.target: Confident prototype data model available by September 2026.

measures.title: Prototype usability
measures.questionAnswered: Can faculty and programme users understand and use the prototype insight pack?
measures.measure: Feedback from pilot users on clarity, relevance, interpretability and usefulness for programme sense-making.
measures.baseline: No reusable programme-level insight pack currently exists.
measures.target: Pilot users confirm that the pack is sufficiently clear and useful to support structured conversations.

measures.title: Responsible interpretation
measures.questionAnswered: Are facilitators and programme teams handling small numbers, suppression, comparator data and marginal destinations appropriately?
measures.measure: Use of caveat guide, facilitator feedback and review of completed reflection templates.
measures.baseline: Interpretation risks are not yet systematically controlled.
measures.target: Faculty facilitators use the caveat guide consistently during priority rollout.

measures.title: Faculty facilitator readiness
measures.questionAnswered: Are faculties ready to run programme sense-making locally without central facilitation for every programme?
measures.measure: Number of faculties with trained facilitators, confirmed rollout plans and access to toolkit materials.
measures.target: All faculties have identified facilitators and rollout plans before priority wave delivery.

measures.title: Programme engagement and sense-making
measures.questionAnswered: Are programmes using the insight packs to reflect on strategy, not just outcomes?
measures.measure: Completed programme or department reflection templates, workshop records and faculty returns.
measures.baseline: Programme-level engagement with graduate futures data is inconsistent.
measures.target: Priority programme, department or cluster conversations completed by September 2027, with full coverage targeted by September 2028.

measures.title: Action orientation
measures.questionAnswered: Does the process lead to realistic programme-level actions with owners and support needs?
measures.measure: Quality and completeness of Graduate Futures Action Plans, including named owners, support asks and monitoring points.
measures.baseline: Programme-level employability actions are not consistently derived from a shared graduate futures evidence base.
measures.target: Completed action plans for programmes included in each rollout wave.

measures.title: Story activation
measures.questionAnswered: Can programmes generate evidence-informed celebratory language for open days and student-facing use?
measures.measure: Story Packs produced, programme confirmation recorded and evidence-informed messages used in local recruitment or student-facing contexts.
measures.baseline: Programme-level graduate futures stories are inconsistent and often not linked to evidence.
measures.target: Story Pack content available for programmes included in each rollout wave.

measures.title: Institutional learning and feed-through
measures.questionAnswered: Is King's building a clearer institutional typology of graduate futures patterns, programme strategies, strengths and support needs?
measures.measure: Institutional synthesis produced and used to inform related King's Edge deliverables, Careers planning, curriculum enhancement and graduate premium evidence work.
measures.baseline: Institutional understanding of programme-level graduate futures patterns and strategy types is underdeveloped.
measures.target: First institutional synthesis completed after the priority rollout, with fuller synthesis following full coverage.

definitionOfDone: The data source inventory, caveat guide, destination taxonomy and comparator framework are completed for phase one.
A confident Power BI or equivalent insight pack prototype is ready by September 2026, with core data in place and presentation co-constructed with users.
The Programme Graduate Futures Insight Pack template is tested with real programme, department or subject examples.
The train-the-trainer toolkit is produced and faculty facilitators are prepared to run local sense-making conversations.
Priority programme, department or cluster conversations are completed by September 2027, with full programme coverage targeted by September 2028.
Graduate Premium Profile, Graduate Futures Action Plan and Graduate Futures Story Pack templates are in use.
Heads of Department are confirmed as benefit owners and action plan follow-up remains internal to departments and faculties.
Programme confirmation is recorded as sufficient approval for Story Pack use.
Faculty returns are synthesised into an institutional typology of graduate futures patterns, programme strategy types, strengths and support needs.
Findings are fed into related King's Edge work, including graduate premium evidence packs, market activation, skills work and curriculum enhancement.

resources.fundingSummary: Requires alignment of Careers and Employability Service data capacity, Power BI development, programme delivery coordination and faculty facilitator time. Additional or protected resource is likely needed, potentially including a sandwich year placement to support production, coordination and synthesis.

existingCapacity.contribution: Own or co-own the data product, provide employability expertise, support interpretation and contribute to faculty-facing guidance.
Provide institutional graduate futures leadership, maintain coherence with King's Edge and own institutional synthesis.
Lead delivery of the insight pack model, toolkit development, rollout coordination and product integration.
Act as decision forum and faculty delivery route for the train-the-trainer cascade.
Own benefit realisation and ensure programme actions are followed up through internal department and faculty planning routes.
Run local programme or department sense-making conversations using the toolkit.

newInvestment.item: Protected analytical and Power BI build capacity
newInvestment.rationale: Needed to produce the confident September 2026 prototype and maintain the insight model.

newInvestment.item: Sandwich year placement or equivalent coordination resource
newInvestment.rationale: Could support data source inventory maintenance, pack preparation, testing, faculty return tracking and synthesis, without being the critical owner of the analytical model.

newInvestment.item: Light content design or editorial support
newInvestment.rationale: May be needed to ensure profiles, action plan templates and story pack templates are clear, usable and appropriately celebratory.

enablingConditions.condition: Access to graduate outcomes, LEO, TEF, benchmarking, enrolment supplement and relevant internal datasets.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The prototype cannot provide a credible programme-level graduate futures view.

enablingConditions.condition: Power BI prioritisation or equivalent data visualisation route.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The insight pack may remain static or manually produced, limiting scalability.

enablingConditions.condition: Faculty agreement to use the train-the-trainer model.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The central team may become a bottleneck and programme coverage will not be achievable.

enablingConditions.condition: Heads of Department accept benefit ownership.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Action plans may be produced but not owned or followed up.

enablingConditions.condition: Programme confirmation is accepted as sufficient approval for Story Pack content.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Story Pack activation may become too slow or over-governed.

investmentAsk.rationale: The September 2026 prototype and subsequent faculty cascade require protected analytical, Power BI and coordination capacity. Without this, the project may be conceptually strong but operationally under-resourced.

steps.title: Build the data foundation and prototype specification
steps.summary: Confirm data sources, data ownership, caveats, comparator principles, destination taxonomy and the minimum viable insight pack specification for the September 2026 prototype.

outputs.title: Phase one data source inventory
outputs.summary: Documents available data, owners, caveats and readiness for prototype use.

outputs.title: Destination taxonomy
outputs.summary: Defines graduate, non-graduate, marginal, transitional and pathway destinations.

outputs.title: Comparator framework
outputs.summary: Defines meaningful subject, sector and Russell Group comparator principles.

existingCapacity.contribution: Lead data product ownership and interpretation.

existingCapacity.role: Planning Analytics or relevant data colleagues
existingCapacity.contribution: Support access to institutional data and benchmarking sources.

newInvestment.rationale: Needed to prepare the prototype data model at pace.

enablingConditions.condition: Phase one data sources are accessible and approved for use.

decisions.title: Confirm phase one data sources
decisions.summary: Decide which data sources must be included in the September 2026 prototype.

decisions.title: Confirm Power BI route
decisions.summary: Decide whether the prototype is built directly in Power BI or as an interim insight pack with Power BI build in parallel.

risks.title: Data scope becomes too ambitious for September 2026
risks.mitigation: Lock a minimum viable prototype and maintain a future enhancement list.

assumptions.title: Core data availability
assumptions.summary: Core datasets are available, with the main challenge being presentation, integration and interpretation.

steps.title: Produce the confident insight pack prototype
steps.summary: Create a September 2026 prototype insight pack with data in place, Power BI presentation developing and user-facing presentation co-constructed with faculties and programmes.

outputs.title: Confident September 2026 prototype
outputs.summary: A usable prototype insight pack that can support real programme conversations.

outputs.title: Data caveat and interpretation guide
outputs.summary: Guidance for responsible use of data, comparators, segmentation and destination classifications.

existingCapacity.contribution: Lead data interpretation and prototype development.
Build or specify the prototype dashboard and pack presentation.

newInvestment.rationale: Needed to create a usable, scalable prototype rather than a manually produced static report.

enablingConditions.condition: Prototype is tested with representative programme or department examples.

decisions.title: Confirm prototype readiness standard
decisions.summary: Agree that the September 2026 milestone means a confident prototype with data in place, not a fully finalised dashboard.

risks.title: Prototype is technically complete but not usable
risks.mitigation: Co-construct presentation with programme and faculty users before locking the pack design.

assumptions.title: Co-construction improves adoption
assumptions.summary: Faculty and programme input into presentation will improve usability and reduce defensive interpretation.

steps.title: Train faculty facilitators and complete priority rollout
steps.summary: Use a train-the-trainer model to prepare faculty facilitators and complete the first priority wave of programme, department or cluster conversations by September 2027.

outputs.title: Train-the-trainer toolkit
outputs.summary: Facilitator guide, training deck, reflection prompts, caveat guidance and structured return templates.

outputs.title: Priority programme conversations
outputs.summary: First wave of programme, department or cluster sense-making sessions completed through faculty delivery.

outputs.title: Draft profiles, action plans and story packs
outputs.summary: Programme outputs captured from the priority rollout.

existingCapacity.contribution: Provide decision route and faculty delivery authority.

existingCapacity.role: Associate Directors of Education and faculty education leads
existingCapacity.contribution: Facilitate or coordinate local sense-making conversations.
Support data interpretation and employability strategy conversations.

newInvestment.item: Sandwich year placement or coordination support
newInvestment.rationale: Could support pack production, workshop logistics, return tracking and synthesis.

enablingConditions.condition: Faculties identify facilitators and priority rollout coverage.

decisions.title: Confirm priority rollout scope
decisions.summary: Decide which programmes, departments or clusters are included in the September 2027 priority wave.

decisions.title: Confirm faculty facilitator model
decisions.summary: Decide which roles facilitate locally and how they are trained.

risks.title: Faculty cascade is uneven
risks.mitigation: Use a standard toolkit, clear Vice Dean ownership and structured return templates.

risks.title: Programme teams experience the work as performance management
risks.mitigation: Train facilitators to frame the process as developmental, comparative and action-oriented.

assumptions.title: Central team will not facilitate every workshop
assumptions.summary: The model depends on faculty-led delivery, with central support focused on method, training, data and synthesis.

steps.title: Complete full coverage and institutional synthesis
steps.summary: Extend the faculty-led model to full programme coverage by September 2028 and synthesise institutional patterns, strengths, support needs and programme strategy types.

outputs.title: Full programme coverage
outputs.summary: Programme sense-making completed across the agreed full coverage population by September 2028.

outputs.title: Institutional graduate futures typology
outputs.summary: Synthesis of outcome patterns, destination pathways, strategy types, strengths and support needs.

outputs.title: Feed-through to related King's Edge work
outputs.summary: Findings used to inform evidence packs, market activation, skills work and curriculum enhancement.

existingCapacity.contribution: Own benefit realisation and internal follow-up of action plans.
Lead institutional synthesis and connection to the wider graduate futures agenda.
Embed follow-up through internal faculty and department planning routes.

newInvestment.rationale: Needed to manage structured returns and produce institutional learning from the full rollout.

enablingConditions.condition: Action plan follow-up remains internal to departments and faculties.
Programme confirmation is accepted as sufficient for Story Pack use.

decisions.title: Confirm full coverage definition
decisions.summary: Agree whether full coverage means every programme, every department or every agreed programme cluster.

decisions.title: Confirm synthesis route
decisions.summary: Agree where institutional findings are reported and how they feed related Edge deliverables.

risks.title: Action plans are produced but not followed up
risks.mitigation: Confirm Heads of Department as benefit owners and connect actions to internal department and faculty planning routes.

risks.title: Story Packs drift into unsupported marketing claims
risks.mitigation: Require evidence-linked statements and programme confirmation before local use.

assumptions.title: Full coverage by September 2028 is feasible through faculty-led rollout
assumptions.summary: The target depends on faculties owning local scheduling, facilitation and follow-up.

dependencies.label: Feeds programme-level graduate futures insight into Graduate Premium Evidence Packs.
dependencies.description: The insight packs, profiles and institutional synthesis provide evidence and programme-level material for graduate premium evidence packs.

dependencies.label: Feeds Story Pack content into graduate premium engagement and market activation.
dependencies.description: Story Pack language and proof points can support open days, recruitment, induction and market-facing narratives.

dependencies.label: Uses skills demand intelligence and connects to the Standard Skills Classification work.
dependencies.description: This deliverable should use skills demand intelligence where relevant, but the full skills architecture remains in 2.1.3.

dependencies.label: May connect insight and action planning to programme review and curriculum enhancement.
dependencies.description: Action plans and programme strategy findings should be available to inform existing education planning and curriculum enhancement routes.

risks.title: September 2026 prototype deadline is missed
risks.description: The data product may become too ambitious or under-resourced to produce a confident prototype by September 2026.
risks.mitigation: Lock minimum viable phase one data sources, use a confident prototype standard rather than a fully final dashboard standard, and secure protected analytical or Power BI capacity.

risks.title: The work becomes too centrally resource-intensive
risks.description: Central teams cannot facilitate programme conversations for every programme.
risks.mitigation: Use the train-the-trainer model, with faculty-led facilitation and central support focused on data, method, templates, training and synthesis.

risks.title: Data is over-interpreted or used punitively
risks.description: Small numbers, suppression, volatility, coding issues or comparator misuse could lead to unfair or unhelpful interpretations.
risks.mitigation: Use the data caveat guide, facilitator training, destination taxonomy and responsible interpretation principles.

risks.title: Marginal destinations are misread as simple failure
risks.description: Destinations coded as non-graduate may include transitional or near-graduate pathways that require interpretation.
risks.mitigation: Create and use a destination taxonomy that distinguishes graduate, non-graduate, marginal, transitional and pathway destinations.

risks.title: Faculty rollout is inconsistent
risks.description: Cascaded delivery may vary in quality, tone and completion across faculties.
risks.mitigation: Use a common toolkit, Vice Dean decision route, facilitator training, structured returns and progress tracking.

risks.title: Action plans are not followed up
risks.description: Programme action plans may be created but not owned or monitored after the workshop.
risks.mitigation: Confirm Heads of Department as benefit owners and route follow-up through internal department and faculty planning processes.

risks.title: Story Pack content becomes unsupported marketing language
risks.description: Open day or recruitment language could drift beyond the evidence base if not handled carefully.
risks.mitigation: Require evidence-linked proof points and programme confirmation before Story Pack use.

issues.title: Data product resourcing needs confirmation
issues.description: Careers and Employability Service can act as data product owner, but additional or protected capacity may be needed, potentially including a sandwich year placement.

issues.title: Full coverage definition still needs to be locked
issues.description: The target is full programme coverage by September 2028, but the precise unit of coverage needs confirmation: programme, department, pathway or agreed programme cluster.

assumptions.title: Core data is available
assumptions.summary: The key data sources are available, with the main delivery challenge being presentation, integration, interpretation and usability.

assumptions.title: Prototype can be confident without being final
assumptions.summary: The September 2026 milestone is a confident prototype with data in place, not a fully finalised dashboard.

assumptions.title: Faculty-led delivery is necessary for scale
assumptions.summary: The central team cannot deliver programme workshops for every programme, so faculties must facilitate through a train-the-trainer model.

assumptions.title: Programme confirmation is enough for Story Pack use
assumptions.summary: Programme teams can confirm that Story Pack language is accurate, fair and evidence-informed before local use.

assumptions.title: Action plan follow-up remains internal
assumptions.summary: Action plans should be followed up through department and faculty planning routes, rather than becoming a central performance-management process.

decisions.title: Confirm protected resource for data product development
decisions.description: Decide whether additional analyst, Power BI or coordination capacity is required, including whether a sandwich year placement should be pursued.
decisions.decisionMaker: Vice Deans Education or delegates, with Careers and Employability Service

decisions.title: Lock the September 2026 prototype scope
decisions.description: Confirm the minimum viable data sources, pack structure, comparator framework and destination taxonomy required for a confident prototype.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace, Daniel Robson and Careers and Employability Service

decisions.title: Confirm priority rollout scope
decisions.description: Decide which programmes, departments or clusters are included in the priority wave to September 2027.

decisions.title: Confirm full coverage definition
decisions.description: Decide what counts as full programme coverage by September 2028.

decisions.title: Confirm institutional synthesis route
decisions.description: Agree where the institutional typology and synthesis will be reviewed and how findings feed related King's Edge deliverables.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace and Vice Deans Education or delegates
